memes.label: "The Gambler · Contents"
memes.description: "Table of contents for The Gambler, listing all 30 sections across 17 chapters."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 1"
memes.description: "The Gambler by Fyodor Dostoyevsky Translated by C. J. Hogarth Contents Chapter I Chapter II Chapter III Chapter IV Chapter V Chapter VI Chapter VII Chapter VIII"

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 2"
memes.description: "“But—” the General began. “What really saved you was the fact that you proclaimed yourself a heretic and a barbarian,” remarked the Frenchman with a smile."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 3"
memes.description: "When, on the present occasion, I entered the gaming-rooms (for the first time in my life), it was several moments before I could even make up my mind to play."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 4"
memes.description: "“Why not?” she asked excitedly. “Because I wish to play _for myself_,” I replied with a feigned glance of astonishment."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 5"
memes.description: "“That Mlle. Blanche,” he said. “Well, I have seen a good many women like her.” After that he was silent as he looked me meaningly in the face."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 6"
memes.description: "“How far you may be right in what you have said I do not know,” remarked the General moodily; “but I _do_ know that you are becoming an insufferable _farçeur_…"

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 7"
memes.description: "“What stupid rubbish!” she cried. “I care not whether it be wise or stupid,” I cried in return. “I only know that in your presence I must speak, speak, speak."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 8"
memes.description: "Wheeling quietly about, I returned in the direction of Polina Alexandrovna."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 9"
memes.description: "Yet it was not the General but Polina that I wanted to anger."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 10"
memes.description: "“Very well, then,” he said, in a sterner and more arrogant tone."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 11"
memes.description: "“Enough! You need say no more,” I cried with a strange emotion at my heart, yet not altogether understanding what had aroused that emotion in my breast."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 12"
memes.description: "“I know of nothing else,” asserted Mr. Astley doggedly. “But, I do, I do!” I shouted in my fury."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 13"
memes.description: "“Oui, madame,” assented De Griers. “Et, croyez, je suis si enchanté! Votre santé—c’est un miracle vous voir ici. Une surprise charmante!” “Just so."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 14"
memes.description: "For instance, on one occasion she halted before a picture which, a poor copy of a well-known original, had a mythological subject."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 15"
memes.description: "In the last resort they always called in the Casino police, and the disputes would immediately come to an end."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 16"
memes.description: "I hastened to translate. “Oui, Madame,” was the croupier’s polite reply. “No single stake must exceed four thousand florins."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 17"
memes.description: "All these thoughts passed through my mind during the few moments that, having left the old lady’s rooms, I was ascending to my own room on the top storey."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 18"
memes.description: "It happened thus: As soon as ever we arrived the Grandmother ordered me to stake twelve ten-gülden pieces in succession upon zero."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 19"
memes.description: "The wheel revolved. “Zero!” cried the croupier. We had lost it all! “The fool!” cried the old lady as she turned upon De Griers."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 20"
memes.description: "I failed to find Mr. Astley, and returned home."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 21"
memes.description: "Meanwhile, that day some other very important events were passing in our hotel."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 22"
memes.description: "A minute later, though sobbing and almost breathless, he managed to gasp out that Mlle."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 23"
memes.description: "Her eyes flashed fire. “What? _you yourself_ wish me to leave you for him?” she cried with a scornful look and a proud smile."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 24"
memes.description: "Yet some whim or other led me, on remarking that the red had come up consecutively for seven times, to attach myself to that colour."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 25"
memes.description: "“Well? Are you going to hand me over my fifty thousand francs?” “Polina, you say that _again, again?_” I exclaimed."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 26"
memes.description: "For a moment I reflected. “No!” I shouted. “My account, please, for in ten minutes I shall be gone.” “To Paris, to Paris!” I added to myself."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 27"
memes.description: "True enough, the marriage took place. It did so at the close of our month together, and I am bound to suppose that it was upon the ceremony that the last…"

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 28"
memes.description: "“I must behave altogether differently,” she confided to me with a serious air."

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 29"
memes.description: "“Well,” he said—not very pleasantly, “I am none the less glad to find that you retain your old independence of spirit, as well as your buoyancy.” “Which means…"

memes.label: "The Gambler · Section 30"
memes.description: "“No, Mr. Astley. After all that has been said I—” “_Take care_ of them!” repeated my friend."

memes.label: Addiction & Fate
memes.description: Index of pages exploring addiction, compulsion, and fatalism — the twin poles of agency and determinism in Dostoyevsky's The Gambler.

memes.label: "Alexei Ivanovitch"
memes.description: "The first-person narrator and protagonist of The Gambler — a young Russian tutor whose compulsive gambling, obsessive love for Polina Alexandrovna, and bitter social consciousness drive the novel's action."

memes.label: "Alexei's Winning Streak"
memes.description: "Alexei Ivanovitch's spectacular winning streak at roulette — the event that briefly transforms him from penniless tutor to possessor of thousands of francs, restructuring all social relationships before the inevitable reversal."

memes.label: The Bank
memes.description: The casino's reserve of money against which players wager, and the institutional structure that ensures the house always wins in The Gambler.

memes.label: Baron Burmergelm
memes.description: A Prussian baron visiting Roulettenberg with his wife, whose humiliating encounter with Alexei Ivanovich in Chapter 7 becomes a pivotal plot catalyst.

memes.label: "The Insult to the Baron"
memes.description: "The social crisis engineered by Polina — Alexei's deliberate insult to the Prussian Baron and Baroness at her instigation, triggering a cascade of confrontations that expose the household's vulnerability to aristocratic opinion and the General's complete powerlessness."

memes.label: "Baroness Wurmerhelm"
memes.description: "A minor but crucial figure in The Gambler — the Prussian aristocrat whose insult by Alexei, engineered by Polina, sets off a chain of social confrontation that exposes the household's precarious position."

memes.label: "Bet"
memes.description: "The fundamental unit of gambling in The Gambler — the act of staking money on an uncertain outcome, which Dostoyevsky uses as a lens to examine risk, desire, class, and self-destruction."

memes.label: Calculation
memes.description: The attempted rational control of chance through calculation and betting systems in The Gambler, and its inevitable failure.

memes.label: "The Casino"
memes.description: "The central setting of The Gambler's action — the casino at Roulettenberg whose roulette tables and trente-et-quarante rooms constitute the novel's primary stage for fortune, ruin, and psychological exposure."

memes.label: Casino Spaces

memes.label: "Chance"
memes.description: "The philosophical force that governs The Gambler's world — the mathematical reality of randomness that characters alternately worship as fortune and rationalise as a calculable system."

memes.label: "Delirium"
memes.description: "The feverish mental state that accompanies obsessive gambling in The Gambler — a form of delirium in which the boundary between calculation and compulsion dissolves."

memes.label: Desire
memes.description: The driving psychological force behind gambling addiction and romantic obsession in The Gambler.

memes.label: "Despair"
memes.description: "A recurring psychological state in The Gambler — the dark emotional undertow that drives characters toward self-destruction at the roulette tables and in their personal relationships."

memes.label: Dignity
memes.description: The fragile social dignity that characters in The Gambler struggle to maintain, and which gambling systematically destroys.

memes.label: "Duel"
memes.description: "The practice of duelling as a social institution of honour among the aristocracy, central to the conflict between Alexei Ivanovich and the Baron in The Gambler."

memes.label: Excitement
memes.description: The heightened emotional state that accompanies gambling — both its seductive appeal and the warning sign of approaching loss of control.

memes.label: "Family"
memes.description: "Family in The Gambler — a web of obligation, secrecy, and financial entanglement that drives the novel's central plot and constrains every major character."

memes.label: "Fate"
memes.description: "The sense of predetermined destiny that haunts The Gambler — a philosophical framework characters invoke to explain both their gambling fortunes and their entrapment in self-destructive patterns."

memes.label: "Fear"
memes.description: "Fear in The Gambler — the emotion that drives characters toward the very outcomes they dread, a force as powerful as desire in the psychology of gambling."

memes.label: Fever
memes.description: The intense physical and psychological excitement of gambling, described as a fever-like state in The Gambler.

memes.label: "Florin"
memes.description: "The Dutch-Austrian gold coin used alongside the gülden in the Roulettenberg casino — a currency that measures the Grandmother's spectacular losses and the novel's international economy of gambling."

memes.label: Folly
memes.description: The self-destructive irrationality of the gambler, a central moral and psychological theme in The Gambler.

memes.label: "Fortune"
memes.description: "The dual concept in The Gambler — fortune as both monetary wealth and the capricious force of luck that the roulette wheel embodies, driving characters between riches and ruin in a single spin."

memes.label: "Franc"
memes.description: "The French currency of international finance and aristocratic intrigue in The Gambler — the medium of De Griers's loans, the General's hopes, and the novel's critique of Franco-Russian economic dependency."

memes.label: "Gambling"
memes.description: "The central activity of The Gambler — a destructive compulsion that consumes the novel's characters and reflects Dostoyevsky's own struggles with addiction."

memes.label: "The Gambling Bank"
memes.description: "The banking institution that underpins the gambling economy of Roulettenberg — the entity that holds the house's capital, issues credit, and processes the cascade of banknotes that circulate through the novel's financial bloodstream."

memes.label: Gambling & Psychology
memes.description: Index of pages at the intersection of gambling mechanisms and psychological themes — how roulette, risk, and obsession intertwine in Dostoyevsky's The Gambler.

memes.label: Gambling Topics
memes.description: Thematic index of all gambling-related content in The Gambler — from roulette mechanics to the psychology of betting, addiction, and financial ruin.

memes.label: Gaming Table
memes.description: The physical gaming table in the Roulettenberg casino — the central stage of gambling action and the object of ritual focus for every player who approaches it.

memes.label: Garden
memes.description: The public gardens of Roulettenberg, a contemplative space mentioned by characters and characteristic of the spa-town setting.

memes.label: "The General"
memes.description: "The retired Russian general whose household forms the novel's social world — a pompous, indebted schemer whose desperate wait for his grandmother's death drives the plot's financial intrigue."

memes.label: The General's Confession
memes.description: The General's forced confession of his debts to the Grandmother after her arrival, revealing the full extent of his financial ruin.

memes.label: "Gentleman"
memes.description: "The social code of gentlemanly conduct and its relationship to gambling — a class distinction that Dostoyevsky both invokes and undermines throughout The Gambler."

memes.label: "Gold"
memes.description: "Gold in The Gambler — the physical form of money that circulates through the casino, embodying both the promise of fortune and the certainty of ruin."

memes.label: "Grandmother"
memes.description: "The General's wealthy grandmother whose unexpected arrival and spectacular gambling spree at Roulettenberg destroy the household's schemes and provide the novel's most memorable comic-tragic set piece."

memes.label: "The Grandmother's Arrival"
memes.description: "The pivotal event in The Gambler — the Grandmother's unexpected appearance in Roulettenberg when she was presumed dying in Moscow, collapsing the General's inheritance schemes and redirecting the novel's entire dramatic trajectory."

memes.label: The Grandmother's Death
memes.description: The death of the Grandmother in Moscow, reported in the novel's final chapters, which finally resolves the inheritance question but too late to save the General.

memes.label: "The Grandmother's Gambling Spree"
memes.description: "The novel's dramatic centrepiece — the Grandmother's sustained roulette session in which she loses a fortune over several days, destroying her wealth and the General's inheritance in a single extended sequence of comic-tragic self-destruction."

memes.label: "Gülden"
memes.description: "The principal currency used in the Roulettenberg casino — a German-Dutch gold coin that measures every character's fortune and ruin in The Gambler."

memes.label: Habit
memes.description: The compulsive habitual cycle of gambling that traps characters in The Gambler, transforming choice into necessity.

memes.label: "Homburg"
memes.description: "The real German spa town mentioned as Alexei's next destination — a real-world counterpart to fictional Roulettenberg and one of Dostoyevsky's own gambling haunts."

memes.label: "Honour"
memes.description: "The social code of conduct that governs—and strangles—The Gambler's characters, a fragile currency traded, pledged, and devalued in the novel's compressed hotel society."

memes.label: "Hope"
memes.description: "The irrational engine of gambling and the fragile emotional state that sustains — and then devastates — every character in The Gambler."

memes.label: "The Hotel"
memes.description: "The hotel at Roulettenberg where the General's household resides — a compressed social space whose corridors, drawing-rooms, and verandahs stage the novel's domestic drama between casino visits."

memes.label: "Inheritance"
memes.description: "The expectation of inheritance that structures the financial and social calculations of every character in The Gambler — the deferred wealth that drives the General's desperation and sets the plot in motion."

memes.label: "Losing"
memes.description: "The inevitable terminus of gambling in The Gambler — not merely the loss of money but the loss of identity, status, and self-control that the roulette table extracts from every player who stays long enough."

memes.label: "Loss"
memes.description: "The inevitable outcome of gambling in The Gambler — loss as the mechanism that strips characters of money, dignity, and self-control, and the engine that paradoxically drives them back to the table."

memes.label: "Love"
memes.description: "The destructive force of romantic love in The Gambler — Alexei's obsessive devotion to Polina, the General's humiliating infatuation with Mlle. Blanche, and Dostoyevsky's portrait of love as a form of gambling that no one wins."

memes.label: "Luck"
memes.description: "The gambler's belief in a personal relationship with chance — that fortune can be courted, recognised, and ridden like a wave — and its inevitable dissolution at the roulette table."

memes.label: Madame de Cominges
memes.description: The mother of Mlle. Blanche de Cominges, a widow who accompanies her daughter in the scheme to marry the General for his expected inheritance.

memes.label: "Marfa"
memes.description: "The Grandmother's maid who accompanies her to Roulettenberg — a peripheral figure whose silent presence reinforces the feudal household structure that the Grandmother represents."

memes.label: "Maria Philipovna"
memes.description: "The General's quiet wife — a background figure in The Gambler whose silent presence and nervous deference define the household's emotional atmosphere."

memes.label: "Marquis de Griers"
memes.description: "The French aristocrat at Roulettenberg — a calculating manipulator whose claims to Polina Alexandrovna's hand and his schemes against the General's fortune represent the novel's critique of European sophistication."

memes.label: "Marriage"
memes.description: "Marriage as a financial transaction and social strategy — the institution that drives the central plot of The Gambler, where romantic unions are inseparable from debt, inheritance, and class ambition."

memes.description: Complete index of all wiki entries for The Gambler, organised by type — characters, concepts, events, places, and organisations.

memes.label: Method
memes.description: Betting methods and systems used by gamblers in an attempt to defeat chance at the roulette tables in The Gambler.

memes.label: Mischa
memes.description: The General's young son, a barely-glimpsed child whose existence underscores the domestic cost of the adults' gambling obsession.

memes.label: "Mlle. Blanche"
memes.description: "The French courtesan at Roulettenberg — a professional adventuress whose calculated pursuit of the General's (anticipated) fortune represents the novel's satire of European social ambition."

memes.label: "Money"
memes.description: "The raw material of every transaction in The Gambler — whether financial, social, or emotional — and the force that reveals the true nature of each character."

memes.label: Money & Class
memes.description: Index of pages exploring money, social class, and their corrosive effects in The Gambler — from aristocratic debt to the roulette table's levelling power.

memes.label: Moscow
memes.description: The Grandmother's home city and the source of her vast estate; a looming presence in the financial calculations of the General's household.

memes.label: Most Cited Pages
memes.description: Pages in The Gambler wiki ranked by total citation count — the entities, concepts, and places most frequently referenced across all entries.

memes.label: "Mr. Astley"
memes.description: "The English gentleman at Roulettenberg — a shy, honourable observer whose quiet devotion to Polina Alexandrovna and moral clarity provide the novel's ethical counterpoint to Russian passion and French calculation."

memes.label: Nadia
memes.description: The General's youngest child, a little girl who appears only as a background figure but whose presence illuminates the household's domestic fragility.

memes.label: "Obsession"
memes.description: "The psychological engine of The Gambler — the all-consuming fixation that transforms roulette from a pastime into the organising principle of Alexei Ivanovich's existence."

memes.label: "Paris"
memes.description: "The French capital referenced throughout The Gambler — the city the Russian characters have left and to which Mlle. Blanche and de Griers wish to return, representing European sophistication, financial predation, and the ambiguous desire that governs the novel's social economy."

memes.label: Park
memes.description: The grounds and walking paths surrounding the Roulettenberg casino and hotel, where characters stroll and converse between gambling sessions.

memes.label: "Passion"
memes.description: "The consuming emotional force that drives Alexei's relationship with Polina, blurring the line between love, obsession, and the gambler's compulsive need to wager."

memes.label: Place Index
memes.description: Index of all locations in The Gambler — from the fictional Roulettenberg to real-world cities referenced in the novel.

memes.label: Places & Spaces
memes.description: Index of pages covering physical locations and social spaces in The Gambler — from the casino floor to the garden paths, from Moscow to the fictional Roulettenberg.

memes.label: "Pleasure"
memes.description: "Pleasure in The Gambler — the ambivalent reward of gambling, inseparable from pain, shame, and the risk of self-destruction."

memes.label: "Polina Alexandrovna"
memes.description: "The General's stepdaughter and the object of Alexei Ivanovitch's obsessive love — a proud, enigmatic young woman whose emotional manipulation and hidden vulnerability drive the novel's psychological drama."

memes.label: "Polina's Rejection of Alexei"
memes.description: "The novel's emotional climax — Polina's refusal of the money Alexei has won for her, a gesture that simultaneously rejects his love, his conception of her, and the transaction model of relationships that governs the novel's social world."

memes.label: "Potapitch"
memes.description: "The Grandmother's devoted old servant in The Gambler — a figure of Russian feudal loyalty whose unquestioning service to his mistress provides a quiet contrast to the self-interested scheming of the other characters."

memes.label: Power & Status
memes.description: Index of pages exploring power, social class, servitude, dignity, and inheritance in The Gambler — the vertical axis of Dostoyevsky's social world.

memes.label: Prayer
memes.description: Religious faith and prayer as a counterpoint to the moral chaos of gambling in Dostoyevsky's The Gambler.

memes.label: "Pride"
memes.description: "A defining psychological trait of The Gambler's characters — pride that isolates, wounds, and drives self-destructive behaviour in the novel's compressed social world."

memes.label: "Probability"
memes.description: "The mathematical reality of roulette that every character in The Gambler attempts to outwit — probability as the impersonal force that governs the wheel and exposes the gambler's delusion of control."

memes.label: Psychological Themes
memes.description: Thematic index of psychological themes in The Gambler — obsession, compulsion, desire, delirium, and the inner forces that drive Dostoyevsky's characters toward ruin.

memes.label: "Reason"
memes.description: "The faculty of rational thought in The Gambler — constantly undermined by gambling passion, social pressure, and self-deception, reason is the ground on which Dostoyevsky stages the conflict between calculation and impulse."

memes.label: "Reproach"
memes.description: "The moral-emotional dynamic of accusation and self-blame that structures the relationships between characters in The Gambler — a weapon, a currency, and a form of self-knowledge."

memes.label: "Risk"
memes.description: "The psychological core of gambling in The Gambler — the embrace of uncertainty that distinguishes the gambler from the calculator, and the condition under which every character's true nature is exposed."

memes.label: "Rouble"
memes.description: "The Russian currency that anchors the characters' financial obligations to their homeland — contrasting with the casino's gülden and the French franc in The Gambler's international economy of debt."

memes.label: "Roulette"
memes.description: "The central gambling mechanism and metaphor of The Gambler — a game of pure chance that mirrors the narrator's psychological descent into obsession and serves as the novel's governing image for fate, fortune, and the mathematics of ruin."

memes.label: "Roulette Bets"
memes.description: "The specific betting options available at the roulette table in The Gambler — rouge et noir, pair et impair, manque et passe, and the fateful zéro — each carrying distinct odds and narrative significance."

memes.label: Roulette Mechanics
memes.description: Index of roulette mechanics and betting systems in The Gambler — the rules, strategies, probabilities, and superstitions that govern play at the tables.

memes.label: Roulette Wheel
memes.description: The central mechanical device of roulette; a revolving wheel that determines wins and losses, symbolising fate and the inexorable nature of chance in The Gambler.

memes.label: "Roulettenberg"
memes.description: "The fictional German spa town where The Gambler takes place — a European resort whose casinos, hotels, and international society create the compressed, feverish world in which the novel's gambling drama unfolds."

memes.label: "The Casino (Institution)"
memes.description: "The institutional entity behind the casino at Roulettenberg — the organisation that operates the roulette and trente-et-quarante tables, extends credit, and presides with mathematical certainty over the fortunes of every character in The Gambler."

memes.label: "Ruin"
memes.description: "The ever-present threat and eventual reality of financial destruction that drives the characters of The Gambler toward desperate wagers and moral compromise."

memes.label: Russia
memes.description: Home country of the General, Alexei Ivanovitch, and many other characters; source of their national identity and financial troubles.

memes.label: Salon
memes.description: Social and gaming rooms in the Roulettenberg hotel and casino, where characters converse and gamble.

memes.label: Servant
memes.description: The social role of servants and the psychological condition of servitude that pervades relationships in The Gambler.

memes.label: "Shame"
memes.description: "The pervasive social emotion in The Gambler — the shamefacedness that defines the General, the humiliation that drives Alexei, and the social exposure that the roulette table forces upon every character."

memes.label: Slavery
memes.description: The narrator's self-identification as Polina's "slave" — a psychological condition of voluntary abasement that defines their relationship.

memes.label: Social Critique Themes
memes.description: Index of social critique themes in The Gambler — Dostoyevsky's dissection of European aristocracy, class pretension, national stereotypes, and the moral emptiness of cosmopolitan society.

memes.label: Spa Town
memes.description: The European spa town — a social ecosystem where gambling, leisure, and class anxiety converge, forming the essential backdrop of The Gambler's action.

memes.label: "Spectacle"
memes.description: "The casino as theatre and gambling as public performance — the novel's persistent framing of social life as spectacle, where characters are simultaneously actors and audience."

memes.label: St. Petersburg
memes.description: The Russian imperial capital, where the General's financial affairs and the narrator's personal history are anchored, representing officialdom and metropolitan excess.

memes.label: Stake
memes.description: The amount of money wagered on a roulette turn; a central mechanism of gambling that carries social codes, psychological weight, and symbolic meaning in The Gambler.

memes.label: "Superstition"
memes.description: "The irrational beliefs that gambling engenders in The Gambler — the rituals, hunches, and magical thinking that characters deploy against the mathematical certainty of the roulette wheel."

memes.label: Switzerland
memes.description: A country repeatedly referenced as a travel destination and place of past events; associated with Mr. Astley and the novel's closing passages as a symbol of possible redemption.

memes.label: "Betting System"
memes.description: "The belief in a rational, methodical approach to roulette through systematic betting — a delusion the narrator both adopts and rejects, and a central metaphor for the human attempt to master chance."

memes.label: The Telegram Crisis
memes.description: The flurry of telegrams from Moscow regarding the Grandmother's health, culminating in her unexpected arrival at Roulettenberg instead of her death.

memes.label: The Landlord
memes.description: The functionary of the Hotel de Roulettenberg, whose deference to the Grandmother provides comic relief and social commentary during her arrival.

memes.label: "Trente et Quarante"
memes.description: "The second casino game in The Gambler alongside roulette — a card game of chance that serves as an alternative gambling mechanism and narrative setting."

memes.label: "Winning"
memes.description: "The experience and consequences of winning at roulette in The Gambler — an intoxicating state that promises escape from dependency but ultimately proves as destabilising as loss."

memes.label: "Zero (Zéro)"
memes.description: "The zero on the roulette wheel — the house's mathematical advantage, a symbol of fate's caprice that repeatedly frustrates players in The Gambler."

memes.label: "Zero (Zéro)"
memes.description: "The zero slot on the roulette wheel — the house's mathematical advantage that guarantees eventual ruin, and the novel's most powerful symbol of the void toward which all gamblers hurtle."
